articles.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb on U.S.-Taiwan Trade Deal; Easing Jobless Claims
articles.summary: The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) saw gains due to an easing of jobless claims and a new U.S.-Taiwan trade deal. The trade agreement involves significant Taiwanese investment in the U.S. tech sector and reduced U.S. tariffs on Taiwanese goods. Meanwhile, two Fed officials voiced concerns over a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, warning about potential threats to the central bank's independence.

articles.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Pull Back as Core Inflation Cools; Tariff Revenue Shrinks
articles.summary: The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) both closed down despite encouraging inflation data, with core CPI cooling to 2.6% year-over-year in December. While the inflation figure was positive, the Federal Reserve is still expected to hold interest rates steady due to the rate remaining above their 2% target, with Fed official Alberto Musalem noting little reason for near-term cuts. Meanwhile, the U.S. budget deficit reached a monthly record in December, partly due to falling tariff revenues, although adjustments reveal a year-over-year decrease in the deficit.
